abstract: Hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) is liver cancer that starts in the cells that make up the body of the liver. Patients identified emotional distress, loss of independence, disruption to employment and family life, and reduction in quality of life as the negative impact of HCC. In Canada, the estimated incidence of liver and intrahepatic bile duct cancer was 4,700 new cases, and the estimated mortality was 3,500 deaths in 2023.
trial_name: Atezolizumab plus bevacizumab versus sorafenib in unresectable hepatocellular carcinoma
